content: If we frame the open-ended search for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures as a curiosity-driven reinforcement learning problem itself, the fragmented, search-incomplete existing knowledge about both topics becomes the agent’s own uncurated long-term memory. A curiosity-driven active learning agent could prioritize exploring the most uncertain gaps in our existing understanding of agent memory design, rather than just refining already well-mapped architecture approaches.
